axe.garo: Rua (⚠ also means 'to pour' as verb)

king.garo: Books, paper, leaves, flat / thin objects

i saw the film last week..garo: Ang·a ia film ko nia mija antio.

why are they late for the meeting?.garo: Bisong maina meeting ona ru·ute reba·a?

which indian state has the largest popul.garo: India ni badiya adoko mande bangbata?

can you give me your pen?.garo: Na·a nang pen ko on·a mangenma?

note.garo: Never translate English SVO word order directly into Garo
The rakka (·) is part of the root word, NOT a suffix. Suffixes are clean strings — never prepend · to a suffix. e.g. root=re·ang + suffix=a → re·ang·a

description.garo: books, paper, leaves, flat thin objects

garo.garo: Na·a nang pen ko on·a mangenma?

english.garo: to do / to work / to make

rule.garo: Add -na suffix to pronoun/noun for indirect object

notes.garo: Damo = common spoken wait; Sengbo also valid
Hai = expression particle for collective action
All three map to same Garo word
Don't see only with your eyes
Hoe = yes; mangenva = of course / certainly
Distal demonstrative — differs from Ua (that, nearby)
Baitarong = how many; Baita = how much
Suffix: ang·ming = with me; nangming = with you
In the letter — chitthio = letter+location suffix

rakka_note.garo: The rakka (·) is a phonetic marker belonging to the root word. Suffixes are always clean strings.

_description.garo: Words with multiple meanings depending on context
